key_insight: An autonomous LLM coding agent improved a crystal graph neural network for band-gap prediction by recombining known techniques into a stronger model.
engineering_relevance: This shows LLM agents can help optimize materials-property predictors on standard benchmarks, accelerating model iteration for computational materials discovery.
method: Used a general-purpose coding agent to design and refine a crystal graph network.
Added element-pair features on message-passing edges.
Incorporated a crystal space-group embedding and other known ML methods.
evidence_signal: On the MatBench band-gap benchmark with more than 100k crystals, the agent-produced model trained without external pretraining outperformed all seventeen expert-designed baselines reported for the task.
watch_item: The gain came from implementing established ideas rather than discovering fundamentally new physics-aware architectures, so autonomous search may optimize existing designs more than create novel ones.
paper_title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
